verification_gate: Each adopted capability has source commit and PR, current source path, passing CI receipt, behavioral boundary, known gap, target LifeOS owner, parity gate, and no second implementation row.
proof_summary: ADOPT-001 adopts the proven nu_plugin CodeDB storage capabilities as upgrade inputs, not as duplicate implementation tasks. generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv maps 11 ADOPT-001 capabilities (BlobStore trait, redb source-blob store, PgStore parity, Resumable capture, SHA-256 identity, exact-byte materialization, secret-output guards, bounded read-only MCP, envctl export contract, plus the two extend-class compiler-observed and sandboxed-build capabilities). Every row carries source commit/PR, current source path, passing CI receipt, behavioral boundary, known gap, target LifeOS owner, and parity gate; each capability appears exactly once (no second implementation row). Store authority is not decided here: the store_role column marks every capability as deferred-to-STORE-001, and a dedicated 'Final store authority' row (adopt_task STORE-001) records the deferral. Provenance is inherited faithfully from generated/recovery_adoption_map.csv, the Complete RECOVERY-004 artifact. The map gate holds; no product execution or nu_plugin mutation was performed.

live_commands.purpose: Validate ADOPT-001 map gate: all columns present, no duplicate capability row, store role deferred
